content.claim: Parental consent is the default trigger for SNI assessment, but institutional override exists: if a parent does not cooperate and this endangers the child, assessment can proceed via administrative procedure without consent, initiated by the tankerületi központ (school-district center) or through child-welfare involvement.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: The threshold definition of 'endangers the child' is not precisely specified in available sources
Frequency and outcome distribution of administrative-override assessments is not quantified
No data on parental awareness of override provisions or appeal success rates following override initiation
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
